success-scenarios.scenario-2: If answer or key-exchange emoji appear but no green media packet follows, the voice classifier may stay in `signaling-only stall` as long as it says why that does not prove governed media success.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: If the operator reports a repeated key-exchange stall and the bounded capture packet completed, the classifier may still stay `signaling-only stall` as long as it keeps the capture packet attached.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The voice packet is treated as generic failure text without a bounded media classification.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The voice classifier must explicitly choose among signaling-only stall, governed-media progress, or another bounded app-facing media behavior.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The voice classifier must preserve the bounded capture packet when the app symptom repeats an earlier key-exchange stall.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'video media|separate from voice|diverges earlier|app-facing' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The video classifier stays separate from voice and states whether video matches or diverges from the voice media layer.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: If video stalls earlier than voice, that earlier boundary is preserved explicitly instead of being merged into the voice classifier.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: If the bounded video packet repeats the same key-exchange stall as voice, the classifier may still choose the same no-go class as long as the video packet keeps its own capture evidence.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The video packet silently inherits the voice classifier.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The video classifier must explicitly compare itself against the voice classifier instead of assuming parity.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The video classifier must preserve its own bounded capture packet even when it converges on the same no-go class as voice.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'proxy behavior|green baseline|older calls packets|Telegram-specific' README.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet explains the new Telegram-specific media boundary without reopening generic transport diagnosis.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison packet states whether voice and video converge on the same app-facing class or diverge by media profile.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet collapses back into generic proxy or transport uncertainty.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The comparison packet must preserve the completed Phase-28 no-go packet and the same-window green transport baseline at the same time.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If voice and video converge on the same no-go class, the comparison packet must still say that each packet carried its own bounded capture evidence.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'handoff|capture|voice|video|comparison|Telegram media evidence' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The evidence packet keeps handoff, capture, voice, video, and comparison sections separate before the final decision.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The evidence packet remains distinguishable from the completed Phase-28 calls packet even if the underlying app-facing no-go class stays unchanged.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new media packet cannot be distinguished from the completed Phase-28 calls packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The evidence packet must preserve separate handoff, capture, voice, video, and comparison sections before the final decision is formed.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The evidence packet must explicitly compare the completed Phase-29 media packet against the completed Phase-28 calls packet.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'Telegram media decision|tested Desktop setup|workaround phase|alternate app topology|no-go' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision stays bounded to the tested Desktop setup and says whether a workaround phase, alternate topology, or continued no-go is justified.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision may keep the setup fully no-go as long as it explicitly says that the transport baseline stayed green and the remaining blocker stayed Telegram-specific.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision widens claims or reopens generic transport diagnosis instead of staying Telegram-specific.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The final decision must remain bounded to the tested Desktop setup and explicitly say whether the next justified phase is a workaround phase, alternate topology, or continued no-go.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The final decision must preserve the green transport baseline while explaining why the completed media packet still remains no-go.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'workaround-only|alternate app topology|Phase-29|green transport baseline' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'tested Telegram Desktop setup|no-go class|Telegram-specific workaround' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis packet freezes future work inside Telegram-specific workaround space without reopening generic transport diagnosis.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The hypothesis packet keeps the completed Phase-29 no-go media packet alive as the only historical baseline for workaround comparison.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new workaround phase silently reuses Phase-29 as if it were a generic transport rerun.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The hypothesis widens claims beyond the tested Telegram Desktop setup before any alternate-topology packet exists.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The hypothesis wording must explicitly preserve the green transport baseline and forbid reopening generic datagram repair in Phase-30.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The hypothesis wording must say that the next question is whether topology changes the Telegram no-go class, not whether transport is still green.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'alternate topology|bounded alternate|Phase-29 Desktop handoff|phase27-probe' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'what changed|what stayed fixed|same-window green' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate topology packet stays explicitly separate from the exact Phase-29 Desktop handoff route.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The packet names exactly which handoff element changed and which transport preconditions remain unchanged.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround wave silently reuses the exact Phase-29 Desktop route and therefore cannot classify topology impact.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The alternate-topology packet changes more than one uncontrolled variable and destroys comparison value.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The handoff profile must preserve the same green `phase27-probe` precondition while changing one bounded app-topology dimension.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround capture packet stays comparable with the completed Phase-29 media packet.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The capture packet makes it possible to separate alternate-topology handoff proof from voice and video outcomes.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround capture packet cannot be compared directly against Phase-29.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The capture packet collapses handoff proof and media outcome into one blended transcript.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'alternate voice|voice attempt|same no-go class|Phase-29' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'separate voice packet|changed the no-go class|reproduced it' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate-topology voice packet states whether the no-go class changed relative to Phase-29.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The voice packet stays bounded to one alternate-topology attempt and one media classification outcome.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate voice packet is missing or collapses into the old Phase-29 voice packet.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The voice packet reopens transport diagnosis instead of staying app-facing above the green baseline.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'alternate video|video attempt|separate from voice|Phase-29' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'video packet|no-go class changed|separate from alternate voice' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate-topology video packet stays separate from the alternate voice packet and states whether the no-go class changed.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The video packet proves whether video diverges earlier, later, or at the same layer as alternate voice.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate video packet silently inherits the alternate voice result.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The video packet cannot be compared against the completed Phase-29 video packet.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'workaround comparison|alternate topology|Phase-29|same no-go class' README.md docs/verification-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'changed the no-go class|reproduced the no-go class|voice|video' README.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet explicitly says whether alternate topology changed the Telegram no-go class or preserved it.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison packet keeps separate voice and video deltas against the completed Phase-29 media packet.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet cannot distinguish Phase-30 from the completed Phase-29 packet.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison packet silently treats one improved media profile as universal success for the tested setup.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The comparison packet must state whether the alternate topology changed the no-go class for voice, video, both, or neither.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'workaround evidence|alternate topology|voice|video|comparison' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround evidence packet keeps alternate handoff, capture, voice, video, and comparison sections separate.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The evidence packet stays directly comparable with the completed Phase-29 packet rather than replacing it.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround packet cannot be distinguished from the completed Phase-29 media packet.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The evidence packet omits either the alternate-topology handoff packet or the comparison packet and therefore cannot support a bounded decision.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'workaround decision|alternate topology helps|still no-go|another Telegram-specific branch' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'tested Telegram setup|bounded decision|Phase-29|alternate topology only' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision says whether alternate topology helps, still stays no-go, or points to another Telegram-specific branch.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision explicitly limits any improvement claim to the tested Telegram setup and the exact alternate topology.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision widens claims beyond the tested Telegram setup or reopens generic transport diagnosis.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision fails to say whether the alternate topology changed the no-go class relative to Phase-29.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The final decision must choose one bounded outcome: alternate topology helps, alternate topology still no-go, or another Telegram-specific branch is needed.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'deeper workaround|Phase-30|green transport baseline|Telegram-specific' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis packet freezes future work inside Telegram-specific app-variant space without reopening topology or transport diagnosis.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The hypothesis packet keeps the completed Phase-29 and Phase-30 no-go classes visible as the historical app-facing baseline.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new phase silently reopens Phase-29 or Phase-30 as if they were incomplete transport or topology reruns.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The hypothesis packet fails to state that future work is limited to one bounded Telegram-specific variant per wave.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The deeper-workaround boundary must remain strictly above the preserved green `phase27-probe` transport baseline.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The app-variant packet changes exactly one Telegram-specific dimension while preserving the same green transport baseline.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The handoff packet says explicitly what changed relative to the completed Phase-30 topology and what stayed fixed from the preserved transport baseline.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The variant packet changes multiple uncontrolled app variables and destroys comparison value.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The app-variant packet silently reuses the completed Phase-30 route without naming a new Telegram-specific dimension.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The handoff packet must preserve the same-window green `phase27-probe` precondition while changing only one bounded Telegram-specific app variable.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The deeper-workaround capture packet stays directly comparable with the completed Phase-29 and Phase-30 packets.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The capture packet keeps handoff evidence separate from the later voice, video, and comparison packets.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new capture packet cannot be compared directly against the earlier Telegram media packets.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The capture packet collapses the app-variant handoff evidence into the later media outcome packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The capture surface must stay reusable for separate voice and video packets under the same app-variant contract.

success-scenarios.scenario-2: The voice packet stays above the preserved green transport baseline and does not reopen generic datagram diagnosis.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The app-variant voice packet collapses into an older voice packet or reopens transport diagnosis.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The voice packet cannot say whether the chosen variant changed the no-go class or reproduced it unchanged.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet explicitly says whether the chosen app variant changed the Telegram no-go class or preserved it.

failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison packet cannot say whether the observed delta came from the single bounded app variant or from uncontrolled setup drift.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'capture topology|127.0.0.1:1080|loopback|uplink|network capture|server correlation' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The capture topology stays exact and preserves the working Desktop baseline while adding attribution evidence surfaces.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The topology changes the preserved baseline or leaves the attribution capture shape ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The capture topology must explicitly name all capture surfaces required for attribution.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The capture topology must explicitly forbid temporary mobile listeners, alternate ports, and stale SSH-forward variants inside the same attribution packet.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'loopback|uplink|network capture|pcap|separate evidence|insufficient evidence' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The workstation-side capture packet can distinguish SOCKS-governed media from traffic that escapes the old envelope.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The capture packet remains too weak and collapses back into UI-only symptom reading.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The capture contract must keep loopback, uplink, and broader workstation network evidence separate.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Missing capture evidence must remain classified as an evidence gap rather than guessed behavior.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The network-capture packet must be strong enough to distinguish SOCKS-governed traffic from workstation traffic that bypasses the preserved SOCKS endpoint.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The server-side contract says exactly what governed-path evidence should appear if Telegram media really enters n0wss.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: Server silence cannot be interpreted because the correlation contract is too vague.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The contract must separate workstation evidence from server-side governed-path evidence.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The contract must let later packets classify server silence honestly.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The server-side packet must say which historical governed markers would count as positive media-path evidence during the same attribution window.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The voice packet yields one bounded attribution class without reopening spent branches.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The voice packet remains a blended UI transcript with no attribution value.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The voice packet must stay separate from video.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The voice packet must correlate workstation and server evidence before any classification is accepted.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The voice packet must say whether the first positive evidence appeared on the SOCKS-governed path, outside it, or nowhere.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The preserved voice packet must classify key-exchange progress as user-visible outcome evidence only; it may not treat key exchange alone as proof of successful audio establishment.

LV-001.NAME: RemoteAccessPreparation
LV-001.goal: Prepare both clean hosts without storing credentials in repository artifacts.

commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash'

required-evidence.evidence-1: The same release binary or checksum is staged for both hosts.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Host roles are fixed: one machine serves WSS, the other serves client SOCKS5 ingress.
required-evidence.evidence-3: No command transcript or log artifact stores the SSH password or raw auth token.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The server host exposes bash, ss, and openssl, and the client host exposes bash, ss, and curl before live staging begins.

LV-001.failure-packet-template: scenario=RemoteAccessPreparation; expected-evidence=both hosts reachable and identified; observed-evidence=ssh or staging failure output; first-divergent-block=HOST_CONNECTIVITY; next-action=repair ssh access or package staging before live launch

LV-002.NAME: TwoHostSmokeExecution
LV-002.goal: Run the first deterministic two-host smoke with one success path and one auth rejection path.

commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'nohup /opt/n0wss/n0wss --auth-token $N0WSS_AUTH_TOKEN client --remote-wss-url wss://$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST/tunnel --tls-trust-anchor-path /opt/n0wss/certs/server.pem >/var/log/n0wss-client.log 2>&1 &'

required-evidence.evidence-1: VF-LIVE-001, VF-LIVE-002, VF-LIVE-003, and VF-LIVE-005 each produce their required log markers or trace sequence evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The smoke request returns deterministic output metadata such as HTTP status, response headers, or a checksum of a known small payload.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The success-path evidence packet includes the command exit code, bounded log tails, and bound-socket state from both hosts.

LV-002.failure-packet-template: scenario=TwoHostSmokeExecution; expected-evidence=live startup, one successful proxy request, and clean shutdown; observed-evidence=log excerpts, command exit codes, socket state, and remote process state; first-divergent-block=first missing required marker; next-action=repair the failing module before repeating the live wave

LV-003.NAME: LiveAuthRejectExecution
LV-003.goal: Run one explicit bad-auth attempt after the success path and prove the server rejects it without creating a live session.

commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'timeout 15 curl --proxy socks5h://127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_BAD_AUTH_SOCKS5_PORT --max-time 10 https://example.com -o /tmp/n0wss-bad-auth.out -sS || true'

required-evidence.evidence-1: VF-LIVE-004 produces the auth validation anchor and a deterministic reject trajectory.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The bad-auth attempt leaves no surviving session on the server after the failed request.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The failure is observable without exposing the invalid token value in logs or saved command transcripts.

LV-003.failure-packet-template: scenario=LiveAuthRejectExecution; expected-evidence=explicit bad-auth attempt rejected with redacted evidence and no surviving session; observed-evidence=server log tail, client log tail, bad-auth command exit code, and session or socket state; first-divergent-block=first missing reject-path marker; next-action=repair auth or handshake handling before any further live rerun

LV-004.NAME: LiveEvidenceCollection
LV-004.goal: Collect narrow high-signal artifacts from both hosts so another agent can diagnose failure without rerunning blind.

commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'tail -n 200 /var/log/n0wss-client.log'

required-evidence.evidence-1: Logs include only anchored high-signal lines and redact secrets.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Socket listings prove whether listeners were bound at the moment of success or failure.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Captured evidence is small enough for a follow-up fix packet and identifies the first divergent block.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The evidence bundle contains separate success-path and bad-auth-path artifacts so the two trajectories are not conflated.

LV-004.failure-packet-template: scenario=LiveEvidenceCollection; expected-evidence=both host logs plus socket state plus smoke result; observed-evidence=missing or noisy artifacts; first-divergent-block=EVIDENCE_COLLECTION; next-action=improve log capture or restart with explicit output paths

LV-005.NAME: ManagedServiceWavePreparation
LV-005.goal: Prepare the managed deployment shape for the second live wave without falling back to ad hoc host mutation.

commands.command-2: systemd-analyze verify deploy/systemd/n0wss-server.service deploy/systemd/n0wss-client.service

required-evidence.evidence-1: The canonical deploy layout, unit names, env sources, and log targets are fixed before managed live verification starts.
required-evidence.evidence-2: No approved command requires raw secret material to be embedded in repository files.

LV-005.failure-packet-template: scenario=ManagedServiceWavePreparation; expected-evidence=deploy script, units, and secret inputs are aligned; observed-evidence=dry-run, static verify, or doc drift failure; first-divergent-block=managed prep artifact mismatch; next-action=repair deploy layout, unit files, or secret inputs before service-managed live execution

LV-006.NAME: ManagedServiceWaveExecution
LV-006.goal: Run the second live wave under managed services with repeated smoke, restart recovery, and bounded bad-auth evidence.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'systemctl restart n0wss-server && systemctl is-active n0wss-server'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'systemctl restart n0wss-client && systemctl is-active n0wss-client'

required-evidence.evidence-1: VF-DEPLOY-002, VF-DEPLOY-003, VF-DEPLOY-004, VF-DEPLOY-005, and VF-DEPLOY-006 each produce their required evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Repeated smoke and restart evidence are captured separately from the bad-auth evidence packet.

LV-006.failure-packet-template: scenario=ManagedServiceWaveExecution; expected-evidence=service-managed readiness, repeated smoke success, restart recovery, and redacted bad-auth reject; observed-evidence=journal, systemctl, socket, and output failure details; first-divergent-block=first missing managed-service marker; next-action=repair service, deploy, or runtime behavior before rerun

LV-007.NAME: TelegramProxyWave
LV-007.goal: Run one Telegram-specific compatibility wave through the governed SOCKS5 endpoint and capture bounded connect plus reconnect evidence.

commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true'
commands.command-3: if Telegram runs on the local operator workstation, start and keep alive `ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT:127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST` before the app-side check
commands.command-4: if Telegram runs on the local operator workstation, prove the forwarded local bind with `ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true` before the app-side check
commands.command-5: telegram-client-side configure SOCKS5 proxy 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT and attempt initial connect
commands.command-6: collect bounded client and server log tails for the initial connect packet
commands.command-7: restart the Telegram app or trigger one reconnect path through the same SOCKS5 settings
commands.command-8: if reconnect stalls at Connecting on the local workstation, verify the SSH-forward process is still alive before classifying a tunnel-side failure
commands.command-9: collect bounded client and server log tails for the reconnect packet

required-evidence.evidence-1: A Telegram app instance is configured as a standard SOCKS5 client against the local n0wss endpoint.
required-evidence.evidence-2: For the local-workstation variant, the packet includes proof of the SSH local forward and the forwarded local bind, not only the remote managed listener.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The success packet contains SOCKS5 parse, transport selection, and pump markers or a bounded failure packet with the first divergent block.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Reconnect evidence is captured separately from the initial connect attempt.
required-evidence.evidence-5: If the app attempt never reaches n0wss, the packet must explicitly classify the divergence as app-side misconfiguration, forward-liveness failure, or readiness failure rather than tunnel failure.
required-evidence.evidence-6: When reconnect succeeds but a local relaunch attempt shows unstable UI recovery, the packet keeps the reconnect-path success evidence separate from the app-launch divergence note.

LV-007.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramProxyWave; expected-evidence=Telegram connects through SOCKS5 and survives one reconnect path with forward proof when the client is local; observed-evidence=Telegram app result, forward state, local listener state, and anchored n0wss logs; first-divergent-block=first missing forward, SOCKS5, or transport marker; next-action=check SSH-forward liveness first for local-workstation runs, then distinguish Telegram misconfig from tunnel-side failure before rerun

LV-008.NAME: FinalTelegramAcceptanceWave
LV-008.goal: Redeploy onto the rebuilt server pair, prove managed readiness, hand off one exact Telegram Desktop path to the operator, and capture separate basic and media-heavy acceptance packets.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-4: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'systemctl is-active n0wss-server && systemctl show n0wss-server -p MainPID --no-pager'
commands.command-5: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'systemctl is-active n0wss-client && systemctl show n0wss-client -p MainPID --no-pager'
commands.command-7: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'journalctl -u n0wss-client -n 100 --no-pager'
commands.command-8: operator starts and keeps alive `ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT:127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST` on the local workstation
commands.command-9: operator proves the forwarded local bind with `ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true` before Telegram Desktop testing
commands.command-10: operator configures Telegram Desktop to use SOCKS5 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT with no username and no password
commands.command-11: operator performs a basic-connect packet and at least one text or small-message action
commands.command-12: operator performs media-heavy checks as available: photo send, large file send, voice call, and video call
commands.command-13: if Telegram stalls at Connecting or a media path fails, verify SSH-forward liveness before classifying a tunnel-side failure
commands.command-14: capture separate bounded server and client log tails after basic-connect and after media-heavy actions

required-evidence.evidence-1: The fresh-host packet proves SSH reachability, OS identity, and baseline tooling on both rebuilt Ubuntu hosts before deploy.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The deploy packet proves the governed managed path was reapplied to both hosts with no ad hoc drift.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The readiness packet proves active services, bound listeners, and startup anchors before the operator starts Telegram testing.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The handoff packet contains the exact SSH-forward command, SOCKS5 parameters, and the moment the operator was told to begin.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The basic-connect packet is separate from photo, large-file, voice-call, and video-call observations.
required-evidence.evidence-6: Any failure packet identifies whether the first divergence was host baseline, deploy, readiness, SSH-forward, SOCKS5 ingress, transport selection, or a media-specific path.
required-evidence.evidence-7: The final go/no-go decision remains explicitly bounded to Telegram Desktop on the rebuilt host pair.
required-evidence.evidence-8: The acceptance record preserves the March 29, 2026 outcome that text, photo, and large-file paths were green while call attempts stalled at Telegram key exchange.

LV-008.failure-packet-template: scenario=FinalTelegramAcceptanceWave; expected-evidence=fresh-host baseline, managed readiness, explicit SSH-forward handoff, basic-connect packet, and separate media packets; observed-evidence=host baseline output, deploy output, service state, forward state, Telegram operator result, and anchored server-client logs; first-divergent-block=first missing host-baseline, readiness, SSH-forward, SOCKS5, transport, or media marker; next-action=repair the first divergent layer before repeating only the dependent acceptance step

LV-009.NAME: TelegramCallsWave
LV-009.goal: Run a dedicated Telegram Desktop voice or video calls wave through SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE and the governed UDP-capable tunnel path.

commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'systemctl is-active n0wss-server'
commands.command-3: operator starts and keeps alive `ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT:127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST` for the local-workstation Telegram Desktop path
commands.command-4: operator proves the local forwarded bind exists with `ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true` before opening the call
commands.command-5: operator configures Telegram Desktop to use SOCKS5 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT and initiates one voice or video call
commands.command-6: capture client and server logs for SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram session open, datagram transport selection, WSS datagram, and UDP relay markers
commands.command-7: capture separate packets for call-setup, media-flow, reconnect, and failure evidence
commands.command-8: end the first call, then trigger one fresh reconnect call attempt through the same SOCKS5 settings
commands.command-9: if the call stalls, separate signaling evidence from UDP media evidence before rerun

required-evidence.evidence-1: Telegram signaling remains green on the existing TCP path while UDP ASSOCIATE creates a governed relay bind.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Outbound and inbound UDP media packets produce bounded datagram transport and relay evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Failure packets classify the first divergent layer as UDP ingress, datagram session, transport selection, remote relay, or app-side behavior.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Reconnect evidence shows whether a new UDP association was opened after the prior call ended or idled out.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The calls wave keeps the SSH-forward liveness proof for the local-workstation variant separate from the UDP media diagnosis.

LV-009.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsWave; expected-evidence=signaling green, UDP ASSOCIATE green, and bounded media-path evidence for one call; observed-evidence=Telegram call result, UDP association state, datagram transport logs, relay logs, and reconnect outcome; first-divergent-block=first missing UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram transport, relay, or inbound media marker; next-action=repair the first divergent layer before replaying the calls wave

LV-010.NAME: TelegramCallsLiveAcceptanceWave
LV-010.goal: Redeploy the UDP-capable baseline to the fresh host pair, hand off one exact Telegram Desktop route to the operator, and collect separate voice, video, reconnect, and failure packets for the live calls setup.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-6: operator starts and keeps alive `ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT:127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST`
commands.command-7: operator proves the local forwarded bind with `ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true` before starting the first call
commands.command-8: operator configures Telegram Desktop to use SOCKS5 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT with no username and no password
commands.command-9: operator runs one voice call and one video call as separate attempts
commands.command-10: capture client and server logs for signaling, UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram transport, WSS datagram, and UDP relay markers during each attempt
commands.command-11: end the first call and run one fresh second call attempt to capture reconnect evidence
commands.command-12: if any call stalls, classify the first missing layer before rerun and keep SSH-forward liveness separate from UDP diagnosis

required-evidence.evidence-1: The live wave yields a fresh-host baseline packet before deployment or app-side interpretation begins.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The readiness packet proves active services, bound listener state, and UDP-capable runtime anchors before the operator starts Telegram calls testing.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The handoff packet contains the exact SSH-forward command, SOCKS5 parameters, and the ordered call-check sequence.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Voice and video results are kept as separate setup or media packets and are not blended.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The reconnect packet shows whether a fresh UDP association was opened after the prior call ended or idled out.
required-evidence.evidence-6: The failure packet identifies the first divergent layer as SSH-forward, UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram session, transport selection, relay, or app-side behavior.
required-evidence.evidence-7: The final go-or-no-go decision remains explicitly bounded to Telegram Desktop on the tested fresh host pair.
required-evidence.evidence-8: Voice and video outcomes remain separately reportable even if one succeeds and the other fails.

LV-010.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsLiveAcceptanceWave; expected-evidence=fresh-host baseline, managed readiness, explicit SSH-forward handoff, separate voice and video packets, reconnect packet, and bounded failure packet; observed-evidence=host baseline output, service state, forward state, Telegram call result, and anchored client-server logs; first-divergent-block=first missing readiness, SSH-forward, UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram transport, relay, or app-side marker; next-action=repair the first divergent layer before repeating only the dependent call step

LV-011.NAME: TelegramCallsLiveRerunAfterIngressFix
LV-011.goal: Redeploy the repaired UDP ASSOCIATE ingress baseline, prove post-fix readiness, and collect a new bounded Telegram calls rerun packet.

commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'systemctl is-active n0wss-client'
commands.command-5: operator reruns one Telegram Desktop voice call through the same SSH-forward and SOCKS5 path
commands.command-6: operator reruns one Telegram Desktop video call through the same SSH-forward and SOCKS5 path
commands.command-7: collect bounded client and server log tails separately for post-fix voice and post-fix video attempts

required-evidence.evidence-1: The rerun packet is explicitly post-fix and tied to the repaired baseline rather than the older Phase-19 deploy.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The rerun proves whether BLOCK_HANDLE_UDP_ASSOCIATE now appears in live logs.
required-evidence.evidence-3: If the first divergent block moved forward, the packet identifies the first missing deeper datagram marker.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Voice and video rerun packets remain separate.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The rerun packet states explicitly whether the ingress defect is fixed even if calls remain no-go overall.

LV-011.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsLiveRerunAfterIngressFix; expected-evidence=post-fix readiness plus a new voice and video rerun packet; observed-evidence=deploy provenance, service state, operator symptom, and bounded log tails; first-divergent-block=first missing post-fix UDP ingress, datagram transport, or relay marker; next-action=repair the first post-fix divergent layer before another live rerun

LV-012.NAME: TelegramCallsLocalClientTopologyWave
LV-012.goal: Run a dedicated Telegram calls wave through a truly local n0wss-client topology and compare its evidence against the older SSH-forwarded Desktop path.

commands.command-1: launch n0wss-client locally on the Telegram Desktop workstation with the bounded local bootstrap profile
commands.command-2: prove the local SOCKS5 listener exists on 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT
commands.command-3: run one local raw SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE probe before Telegram calls begin
commands.command-4: operator runs one Telegram Desktop voice call through the local-client topology
commands.command-5: operator runs one Telegram Desktop video call through the local-client topology
commands.command-6: collect bounded local client logs plus bounded remote server logs for both call attempts
commands.command-7: compare the new local-client packet against the older SSH-forwarded no-go packet before issuing any network-block explanation

required-evidence.evidence-1: The local-client topology packet is kept separate from the older SSH-forwarded packet.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The local wave proves local listener readiness and local UDP ASSOCIATE readiness before Telegram is used.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Voice and video local-client packets remain separate.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final packet compares whether calls behavior changed only because the topology changed.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The wave states explicitly whether any remaining failure is still inside the governed path or only plausibly external to it.

LV-012.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsLocalClientTopologyWave; expected-evidence=local listener proof, local UDP ASSOCIATE proof, separate voice and video packets, and a topology comparison packet; observed-evidence=local bootstrap state, local operator symptom, bounded local client logs, bounded remote server logs, and any governed UDP markers; first-divergent-block=first missing local readiness, local UDP ingress, datagram transport, relay, or app-side marker; next-action=repair only the first divergent local-topology layer before another rerun

LV-013.NAME: TelegramCallsRootCauseIsolationWave
LV-013.goal: Run controlled datagram probes, bounded packet capture, and Telegram Desktop classification so the remaining calls blocker is isolated before any new transport or workaround phase.

commands.command-2: capture a bounded workstation packet or socket trace during one Telegram Desktop voice-call attempt
commands.command-3: capture bounded client and server log tails covering UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram transport, and relay markers during the same attempt
commands.command-4: run one remote media reachability probe through the governed UDP relay path independent of Telegram UI
commands.command-5: assemble a bounded comparison packet covering controlled probe, live app behavior, and remote media reachability

required-evidence.evidence-1: The controlled probe proves or falsifies end-to-end datagram reachability independently of Telegram Desktop.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The workstation capture proves whether Telegram Desktop emits media-path traffic through the proxy-governed path.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The remote media probe proves or falsifies relay reachability independently of Telegram UI state.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final packet separates app behavior mismatch, datagram-path defect, and plausible external filtering.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The wave keeps controlled-probe, live-app, and remote-media packets separate so the first unresolved layer can be promoted cleanly.

LV-013.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsRootCauseIsolationWave; expected-evidence=controlled datagram probe, bounded workstation capture, remote media probe, and bounded root-cause packet; observed-evidence=probe output, capture evidence, runtime markers, and relay evidence; first-divergent-block=first missing controlled-probe, app-behavior, or remote-media marker; next-action=repair or narrow the first unresolved layer before any new workaround phase

LV-014.NAME: DatagramRoundTripIsolationWave
LV-014.goal: Classify the controlled UDP round-trip independently of Telegram UI so outbound and inbound datagram layers can be separated precisely.

commands.command-1: prepare one bounded UDP echo target on the remote host and prove the target port is listening
commands.command-2: run `scripts/udp_probe.sh --socks5 127.0.0.1:1080 --target $N0WSS_UDP_ECHO_TARGET --payload phase23-probe --timeout 5`
commands.command-3: capture one bounded local evidence window covering probe stdout, local runtime markers, and local socket state
commands.command-4: capture one bounded remote evidence window covering the echo target UDP port and relay-side UDP visibility
commands.command-5: assemble one bounded correlation packet naming the first unresolved datagram layer

required-evidence.evidence-1: The echo target packet proves whether the remote UDP port was actually listening during the bounded probe window.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The controlled probe packet distinguishes association success, outbound send, inbound reply, and timeout states.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The correlation packet keeps local and remote evidence separate but time-aligned to one probe attempt.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final packet names the first unresolved datagram layer without reopening Telegram UI or provider-blame ambiguity.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The wave leaves a bounded next-step decision stating whether the next phase is transport repair, relay repair, or app-specific follow-up.

LV-014.failure-packet-template: scenario=DatagramRoundTripIsolationWave; expected-evidence=echo-target readiness, controlled probe output, local and remote bounded capture, and a round-trip correlation packet; observed-evidence=probe status, runtime markers, remote capture state, and timeout or reply result; first-divergent-block=first missing outbound, echo-target ingress, or inbound-reply layer; next-action=repair the first unresolved datagram layer before any new Telegram UI phase

LV-015.NAME: DatagramPathRepairWave
LV-015.goal: Repair and replay the controlled datagram path until the deepest proven layer advances beyond the Phase-23 boundary.

commands.command-1: add bounded dispatch-side instrumentation between local UDP ingress, manager dispatch, transport selection, and WSS datagram emission
commands.command-2: run `cargo test datagram_manager`, `cargo test datagram_transport_selector`, `cargo test datagram`, and `cargo test udp_relay` after the repair edits
commands.command-3: prepare the bounded remote UDP echo target and replay `scripts/udp_probe.sh --socks5 127.0.0.1:1080 --target $N0WSS_UDP_ECHO_TARGET --payload phase24-probe --timeout 5`
commands.command-4: capture one bounded local and one bounded remote evidence window for the post-repair probe
commands.command-5: assemble a post-fix comparison packet against the Phase-23 packet and issue the bounded repair decision

required-evidence.evidence-1: The repair wave adds enough instrumentation to name whether the packet now reaches manager dispatch, WSS emission, server-side relay outbound, or remote echo-target ingress.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The post-repair probe packet states explicitly whether remote ingress or inbound reply has become proven.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The comparison packet says exactly which datagram layer moved compared with Phase-23.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final decision explicitly forbids a new Telegram-specific wave if the controlled datagram boundary did not move.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The post-fix packet keeps the Phase-23 baseline tuple visible so another agent can tell whether `sent` gained deeper evidence or remained ambiguous.

LV-015.failure-packet-template: scenario=DatagramPathRepairWave; expected-evidence=deeper dispatch markers, bounded WSS emission evidence, remote echo-target ingress or explicit absence, and a pre-fix versus post-fix comparison packet; observed-evidence=module-local tests, post-repair probe output, local and remote capture, and repair decision; first-divergent-block=first missing dispatch, WSS emission, relay outbound, or remote-ingress layer; next-action=repair only the first unresolved datagram layer before any new Telegram rerun

LV-016.NAME: DatagramRuntimeGlueWave
LV-016.goal: Prove that repaired datagram helpers are finally wired into the real client and server runtime before any Telegram-specific rerun is attempted.

commands.command-2: cargo test datagram_manager
commands.command-7: prepare the bounded remote UDP echo target and replay `scripts/udp_probe.sh --socks5 127.0.0.1:1080 --target $N0WSS_UDP_ECHO_TARGET --payload phase25-probe --timeout 5`
commands.command-8: capture bounded local and remote runtime evidence for the same runtime-glue probe window
commands.command-9: assemble a comparison packet against Phase-23 and the stopped Phase-24 packet

required-evidence.evidence-1: The runtime-glue wave proves that live client code now reads the UDP relay socket and enters the repaired manager handoff path.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The runtime-glue wave proves that a production WSS datagram carrier emits frames through the real websocket runtime.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The runtime-glue wave proves whether the server-side runtime receives datagram frames and reaches relay outbound handling.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final comparison packet states whether the deepest proven datagram layer moved beyond both the Phase-23 and stopped Phase-24 boundaries.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The wave keeps local receive, manager handoff, production WSS emission, server-side ingress, and remote echo-target ingress as separate evidence classes.
required-evidence.evidence-6: The wave does not allow a helper-only packet or mock-only carrier packet to count as runtime-glue progress.
required-evidence.evidence-7: An inbound timeout is acceptable only after client-side emission and server-side relay outbound are already proven in the same bounded packet.

LV-016.failure-packet-template: scenario=DatagramRuntimeGlueWave; expected-evidence=live UDP runtime receive evidence, production WSS datagram carrier evidence, server-side datagram ingress evidence, and a post-glue comparison packet; observed-evidence=module-local checks, bounded probe packet, local and remote runtime capture, and bounded decision; first-divergent-block=first missing live-runtime, carrier-emission, or server-ingress layer; next-action=repair only the first unresolved runtime glue layer before any Telegram-specific rerun

LV-017.NAME: DatagramInboundReplyWave
LV-017.goal: Repair and prove the bounded inbound datagram return path from remote reply receive through WSS return and local relay delivery before any Telegram-specific rerun is approved.

commands.command-2: cargo test wss_gateway
commands.command-5: prepare the bounded remote UDP echo target and replay `scripts/udp_probe.sh --socks5 127.0.0.1:1080 --target $N0WSS_UDP_ECHO_TARGET --payload phase26-probe --timeout 5`
commands.command-6: capture bounded local and remote runtime evidence for the same inbound-repair probe window
commands.command-7: assemble a comparison packet against Phase-23, superseded Phase-24, and done Phase-25

required-evidence.evidence-1: The wave preserves the Phase-25 baseline that already proves outbound runtime glue through server relay outbound.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The wave proves or rejects server-side inbound receive as a separate layer from outbound relay send.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The wave proves or rejects WSS return and client-side local delivery as separate layers from server-side inbound receive.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final comparison packet states whether the bounded result advanced from `inbound_result=timeout` to `reply-received` or names the next first missing inbound marker.
required-evidence.evidence-5: No Telegram-specific rerun is allowed unless the bounded packet proves the inbound return path far enough for controlled round-trip completion.
required-evidence.evidence-6: The wave keeps reply-source validation evidence separate from successful reply delivery evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-7: The wave must not treat `BLOCK_RELAY_UDP_INBOUND` alone as reply success unless client-side delivery and probe-visible receive are also classified.

LV-017.failure-packet-template: scenario=DatagramInboundReplyWave; expected-evidence=server inbound receive evidence, WSS return evidence, client local-delivery evidence, and a bounded comparison packet against Phase-23 through Phase-25; observed-evidence=module-local checks, bounded probe packet, local and remote runtime capture, and bounded decision; first-divergent-block=first missing server-inbound, WSS-return, or client-delivery layer; next-action=repair only the first unresolved inbound-return layer before any Telegram-specific rerun

LV-018.NAME: DatagramServerInboundReturnWave
LV-018.goal: Repair and prove the server-side inbound receive and return-emission path before any Telegram-specific rerun is approved.

commands.command-2: cargo test wss_gateway
commands.command-4: prepare the deterministic server-local UDP echo target and replay `scripts/udp_probe.sh --socks5 127.0.0.1:1080 --target 127.0.0.1:55123 --payload phase27-probe --timeout 5`
commands.command-5: capture bounded local and remote runtime evidence for the same server-inbound repair probe window
commands.command-6: assemble a comparison packet against the completed Phase-25 and Phase-26 packets

required-evidence.evidence-1: The wave preserves the completed Phase-26 boundary that already proves server-local echo-target ingress but not inbound return.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The wave proves or rejects server-side inbound receive as a separate layer from outbound relay send and target ingress.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The wave proves or rejects server-side WSS return emission as a separate layer from server-side inbound receive.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The wave keeps source-validation evidence separate from reply-return success so a wrong-source reject cannot count as progress.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The final comparison packet states whether the bounded result advanced to server receive, validated WSS return emit, client delivery, or full reply receipt.
required-evidence.evidence-6: No Telegram-specific rerun is allowed unless the bounded packet proves the controlled datagram round-trip far enough for app-facing verification.

LV-018.failure-packet-template: scenario=DatagramServerInboundReturnWave; expected-evidence=server inbound receive, source-validation classification, server return emit, client local-delivery or reply-received evidence, and a bounded comparison packet against Phase-25 and Phase-26; observed-evidence=module-local checks, bounded probe packet, local and remote runtime capture, and bounded decision; first-divergent-block=first missing server-inbound-receive, server-return-emit, or client-delivery layer; next-action=repair only the first unresolved server-side inbound-return layer before any Telegram-specific rerun

LV-024.NAME: TelegramTerminalDecisionWave
LV-024.goal: Decide whether Telegram workaround exploration should stop for the tested variants or whether exactly one genuinely new branch remains.

commands.command-1: rg -n 'Desktop|Android|variant unavailable|alternate topology|mobile|tested variants' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml
commands.command-2: python3 - <<'PY' import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET for p in ['docs/development-plan.xml','docs/knowledge-graph.xml','docs/verification-plan.xml']: ET.parse(p) print(p, 'XML_OK') PY
commands.command-4: rg -n 'Phase-29|Phase-30|Phase-31|Phase-32|stop here|one real remaining branch|127.0.0.1:1080' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml

required-evidence.evidence-1: The wave keeps the preserved green transport baseline and all completed Telegram no-go packets visible together.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The wave explicitly says whether workaround exploration stops here or exactly one real branch remains.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The wave preserves a safe operator cleanup packet with only the working Desktop path left active.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The wave explicitly preserves the full tested-variant matrix and does not let any already-tested branch disappear from the final decision surface.

LV-024.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramTerminalDecisionWave; expected-evidence=final tested-variant matrix plus bounded stop-or-branch decision; observed-evidence=shared-artifact review and final Telegram evidence packet; first-divergent-block=first missing final-decision layer above the preserved green transport baseline; next-action=repair only the terminal Telegram decision surface without reopening generic transport work

LV-025.NAME: TelegramMediaPathAttributionWave
LV-025.goal: Run an attribution-only Telegram media wave that preserves the working Desktop baseline while distinguishing governed-path media, direct-media behavior, signaling-only stall, and insufficient evidence.

commands.command-2: cargo test wss_gateway
commands.command-4: rg -n '127.0.0.1:1080|loopback|uplink|network capture|server correlation|governed-media|direct-media|insufficient evidence' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml docs/OPERATORS.md README.md
commands.command-5: python3 - <<'PY' import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET for p in ['docs/development-plan.xml','docs/knowledge-graph.xml','docs/verification-plan.xml']: ET.parse(p) print(p, 'XML_OK') PY

required-evidence.evidence-1: The wave preserves the working Desktop baseline at `127.0.0.1:1080`.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The wave keeps loopback, uplink, broader workstation network capture, and server-side correlation as separate evidence surfaces.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The wave yields separate voice and video attribution packets.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final classifier and decision stay attribution-only and do not reopen spent workaround branches.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The final packet says whether positive evidence first appeared on the governed path, outside it, or nowhere at all.

LV-025.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramMediaPathAttributionWave; expected-evidence=separate workstation and server attribution evidence plus one bounded classifier; observed-evidence=capture packet set and attribution classification; first-divergent-block=first missing attribution evidence layer above the preserved working Desktop baseline; next-action=repair only the attribution surface or classify the packet as insufficient evidence before any further branch planning

LV-026.NAME: TelegramForcedMediaTopologyWave
LV-026.goal: Run one topology-only Telegram media wave that preserves the normal Desktop baseline outside an isolated runbook while checking whether forced routing changes the direct-media attribution class.

commands.command-2: cargo test wss_gateway
commands.command-4: rg -n 'forced-topology|isolated Telegram|voice|video|both|neither|127.0.0.1:1080' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml docs/OPERATORS.md README.md
commands.command-5: rg -n 'what changed|what stayed fixed|outside controllable user-space envelope|Phase-34' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml docs/OPERATORS.md README.md
commands.command-6: python3 - <<'PY' import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET for p in ['docs/development-plan.xml','docs/knowledge-graph.xml','docs/verification-plan.xml']: ET.parse(p) print(p, 'XML_OK') PY

required-evidence.evidence-1: The wave keeps the normal Desktop baseline untouched outside the isolated forced-topology runbook.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The wave yields separate forced-topology voice and video packets.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The comparison packet says whether the topology changed the class for voice, video, both, or neither.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final decision remains bounded to the exact tested forced topology only.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The wave states one exact isolated topology delta and one exact list of preserved baseline fields from Phase-34.
required-evidence.evidence-6: The wave separately classifies whether first positive evidence appeared on the governed path, outside it, or nowhere for both voice and video.
required-evidence.evidence-7: For snap-based Telegram Desktop builds, the wave proves that the tested process entered the dedicated network namespace through a snap-safe launcher surface instead of `ip netns exec`.

LV-026.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramForcedMediaTopologyWave; expected-evidence=isolated forced-topology handoff plus separate voice/video comparison packet; observed-evidence=topology handoff and forced-topology packet set; first-divergent-block=first missing forced-topology evidence layer above the Phase-34 attribution verdict; next-action=repair only the forced-topology evidence surface or classify the wave as topology no-change before any new branch planning

LV-027.NAME: TelegramTransparentForcedRoutingWave
LV-027.goal: Run one true transparent-routing Telegram media wave that preserves the normal Desktop baseline while checking whether direct media can be forced into the governed envelope.

commands.command-2: cargo test wss_gateway
commands.command-4: rg -n 'Phase-36|transparent-routing|interception|superseded|127.0.0.1:1080' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml docs/OPERATORS.md README.md

required-evidence.evidence-1: The wave preserves the normal Desktop baseline untouched outside the transparent-routing runbook.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The wave proves transparent interception separately from any SOCKS listener or blocked-direct-egress surface.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The wave proves local governed handoff separately from workstation loopback, uplink, broader network capture, and server correlation.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The wave yields separate transparent-routing voice and video packets.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The comparison packet says whether transparent routing changed the class for voice, video, both, or neither against the Phase-34 baseline and the superseded Phase-35 packet.
required-evidence.evidence-6: The final decision remains bounded to the exact tested transparent-routing topology only.

LV-028.NAME: TelegramTransparentHelperWave
LV-028.goal: Run one helper-backed transparent interception wave that proves the helper synthetically before spending Telegram voice and video packets.

commands.command-1: verify the chosen helper package or binary exists or can be installed through the explicit helper runbook
commands.command-2: run the helper install, setup, and cleanup commands from the Phase-37 runbook
commands.command-3: run one isolated synthetic TCP attempt through the helper-backed topology and capture destination plus governed-handoff proof
commands.command-4: run one isolated synthetic UDP attempt through the helper-backed topology and capture destination plus governed-handoff proof
commands.command-5: capture bounded workstation and server evidence for the same helper smoke window
commands.command-6: only after green helper smoke, run one bounded Telegram Desktop voice packet and one bounded Telegram Desktop video packet
commands.command-7: rg -n 'Phase-37|helper-backed|helper smoke|destination recovery|cleanup proof' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml docs/OPERATORS.md README.md docs/technology.xml

required-evidence.evidence-1: The wave proves explicit helper install or bootstrap and explicit helper cleanup.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The wave proves synthetic TCP and UDP helper smoke before any Telegram UI packet is spent.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The wave proves destination recovery and governed handoff separately.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The wave yields separate helper-backed voice and video packets.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The comparison packet says whether the helper changed the class for voice, video, both, or neither against the Phase-34 and blocked Phase-36 baselines.

LV-028.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramTransparentHelperWave; expected-evidence=explicit helper lifecycle, green synthetic TCP and UDP smoke, destination recovery plus governed handoff proof, and separate helper-backed voice and video packets; observed-evidence=helper setup output, synthetic smoke evidence, operator summary, workstation captures, and server correlation; first-divergent-block=first missing install, synthetic TCP, synthetic UDP, destination recovery, governed handoff, voice, video, or comparison marker; next-action=repair only that helper layer before another Telegram packet

LV-029.NAME: TelegramCallsSafeWave
LV-029.scope: Phase-38 safe calls-only branch after the unsafe helper bootstrap path was explicitly superseded.

commands.command-1: keep the preserved normal Telegram Desktop baseline at `127.0.0.1:1080` untouched
commands.command-2: prove the unsafe Phase-37 helper bootstrap path is explicitly superseded
commands.command-3: keep any temporary MTProto or other operator-recovery proxy outside the Phase-38 evidence set
commands.command-4: define one safe calls-only topology and one non-destructive auth/bootstrap contract
commands.command-5: if temporary MTProto was needed for QR or first auth in the safe window, disable it again before judging readiness
commands.command-6: prove the readiness gate before any call packet: no `Connection...`, no `Reconnecting to proxy`, no QR/login screen, and at least one bounded stable dialog surface
commands.command-7: run one safe voice packet
commands.command-8: run one separate safe video packet
commands.command-9: compare the safe calls packets against Phase-34 and unsafe Phase-37 history

required-evidence.evidence-1: The wave keeps ordinary Telegram baseline preservation explicit and separate from new calls work.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The wave never reuses copied live session state from the unsafe helper branch.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Any temporary operator-recovery proxy used to restore the ordinary account is kept outside the calls-only evidence set.
required-evidence.evidence-4: If temporary bootstrap MTProto was needed, it is absent from the readiness and calls packet windows.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The readiness gate is green before any voice or video packet is spent.
required-evidence.evidence-6: Voice and video remain separate packets.

LV-029.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsSafeWave; expected-evidence=preserved ordinary baseline, explicit unsafe-branch supersede packet, safe auth/bootstrap contract, recovery-separation proof, bootstrap-proxy separation proof, green readiness gate, and separate voice/video packets; observed-evidence=phase artifacts, operator evidence, readiness outcome, and safe calls packets; first-divergent-block=first missing safety, recovery-separation, bootstrap-proxy separation, readiness, or calls packet; next-action=repair the first unresolved safe-branch layer before spending more calls attempts

Gate-Phase-1.goal: Foundation modules have deterministic local checks for config, redaction, metrics, auth, and TLS setup.

commands.command-2: cargo test obs
commands.command-7: cargo fmt --check

required-evidence.evidence-1: Startup, metrics, auth, and TLS boundaries have module-local checks defined.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Phase-1 anchors exist for BLOCK_VALIDATE_CONFIGURATION, BLOCK_INIT_TRACING, BLOCK_VALIDATE_HANDSHAKE, BLOCK_LOAD_TLS_MATERIAL, and BLOCK_START_APPLICATION.
required-evidence.evidence-3: No Phase-1 module relies on integration-only transports or end-to-end tunnel setup to prove its local contract.

Gate-Phase-2.goal: Both transport boundaries satisfy the shared adapter cleanup and task-tracking contracts.

commands.command-2: cargo test iroh_adapter

required-evidence.evidence-1: WSS and iroh adapter contract tests pass without leaked tracked tasks.

Gate-Phase-3.goal: Session orchestration, typed effect dispatch, fallback, and state transitions are explicit and verifiable.

commands.command-2: cargo test cli

required-evidence.evidence-1: Registry, selector, pure transitions, and stable top-level effect routing are automated and green.

Gate-Phase-4.goal: SOCKS5 ingress produces bounded ProxyIntent work items and accurate reply codes.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Bounded queue semantics, deterministic request parsing, and exhaustive reply mapping are covered.

Gate-Phase-5.goal: The bridge carries real traffic over transport-agnostic streams.

required-evidence.evidence-1: At least one end-to-end SOCKS5-over-tunnel scenario is automated and green.

Gate-Phase-6.goal: Fallback, shutdown, timing, burst handling, and observability are robust enough for broader execution waves.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Controlled shutdown, queue pressure, session limit pressure, sliding-window burst detection, and transport fallback scenarios produce stable evidence.

Gate-Phase-7.goal: The repository is release-ready for GitHub publication and first-round smoke testing.

commands.command-2: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features

required-evidence.evidence-1: Markup debt is removed, release docs are aligned, CI is current, the release binary is emitted, and the smoke path passes.

Gate-Live-1.goal: The first two-host live environment proves remote readiness, one successful proxied request, one deterministic auth rejection, and clean shutdown.

commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'test -x /opt/n0wss/n0wss'
commands.command-7: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'tail -n 200 /var/log/n0wss-client.log'

required-evidence.evidence-1: VF-LIVE-001 proves server readiness with config, TLS, and startup anchors.
required-evidence.evidence-2: VF-LIVE-002 proves client readiness with config and startup anchors.
required-evidence.evidence-3: VF-LIVE-003 proves one successful proxied request with transport-selection and pump evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-4: VF-LIVE-004 proves invalid auth is rejected with redacted server-side evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-5: VF-LIVE-005 proves shutdown leaves no live listeners or stuck session cleanup.

Gate-Phase-9.goal: The project is ready for repeatable managed deployment, bounded evidence capture, and a second live verification wave.

commands.command-2: systemd-analyze verify deploy/systemd/n0wss-server.service deploy/systemd/n0wss-client.service

required-evidence.evidence-1: Deploy layout, secret handling, deploy scripting, systemd units, and runbook stay aligned.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Managed startup, restart recovery, repeated smoke, and bad-auth checks produce bounded evidence suitable for another agent to debug.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Managed-service evidence includes both system-manager state and runtime anchors, with separate packets for success, restart, and reject trajectories.

Gate-Phase-10.goal: The repository is ready to tag and publish the source-first v0.3.0 release without dirty-tree blockers or release-note drift.

commands.command-2: cargo test

required-evidence.evidence-1: Governed test-file cleanup, release-facing docs, and the v0.3.0 changelog are committed and aligned.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The next release version is explicitly justified against the remote tag baseline.
required-evidence.evidence-3: GRACE review, refresh, and status all support the release-ready decision.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final release-ready commit has no tracked dirty files and no whitespace or patch-integrity defects.

Gate-Phase-11.goal: The project has bounded Telegram-specific compatibility evidence without overstating unblock guarantees.

commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true'

required-evidence.evidence-1: Telegram-facing profile, readiness path, smoke wave, and evidence packet shape are documented and aligned.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Telegram compatibility is documented as SOCKS5 proxy support over the existing tunnel path, not as a new protocol.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Compatibility claims stay inside the verified evidence envelope.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Phase evidence includes separable readiness, initial-connect, reconnect, and bounded-failure packets rather than one blended manual transcript.

Gate-Phase-12.goal: The proven local Telegram Desktop path is operator-reproducible because the SSH-forward prerequisite is explicit in both the runbook and verification contract.

commands.command-1: rg -n 'ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:1080:127.0.0.1:1080|Telegram Desktop|local workstation|remote client host' docs/OPERATORS.md
commands.command-2: rg -n 'LV-007|ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:1080:127.0.0.1:1080|TelegramProxyWave' docs/verification-plan.xml

required-evidence.evidence-2: LV-007 mirrors the same prerequisite instead of assuming a local listener exists by itself.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The knowledge graph stays synchronized with the clarified Telegram Desktop operator path.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The failure contract explicitly checks SSH-forward liveness before a local-workstation Connecting-only symptom can be blamed on the tunnel runtime.

Gate-Phase-14.goal: The rebuilt two-host environment is freshly deployed, operator-ready, and yields a bounded final Telegram Desktop acceptance packet.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'

required-evidence.evidence-2: Both rebuilt Ubuntu hosts are redeployed and green before the operator Telegram test starts.

required-evidence.evidence-4: Basic Telegram acceptance and media-heavy scenarios are recorded as separable packets.

required-evidence.evidence-6: The final go/no-go decision is bounded to the tested Telegram Desktop setup on the rebuilt hosts.

Gate-Phase-18.goal: The project has a governed UDP-capable architecture for Telegram Desktop calls with bounded datagram verification and no overclaim beyond the tested setup.

commands.command-2: cargo test udp_registry
commands.command-7: rg -n 'Telegram|call|voice|video|UDP|SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

required-evidence.evidence-1: The datagram contract, UDP ASSOCIATE ingress, association registry, datagram session manager, and datagram transport path are automated and green.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The Telegram calls wave defines separate signaling, media, reconnect, and failure packets rather than reusing the old TCP-only smoke envelope.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Public claims remain bounded to Telegram Desktop calls on the governed tested setup and do not imply universal unblock behavior.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The initial UDP-capable phase explicitly proves the WSS-backed datagram path and does not silently claim parity for any unimplemented datagram transport backend.

Gate-Phase-19.goal: The fresh host pair is redeployed with the UDP-capable baseline and yields bounded live Telegram Desktop calls evidence without widening the claim beyond the tested setup.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-7: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'journalctl -u n0wss-client -n 100 --no-pager'
